description: Cable types, conduit types, tubing materials, fitting compatibility, seal materials

THHN.not_for: Direct burial, exposed runs, hazardous locations without conduit

MC.MC-HL: For hazardous locations, gas/vapor tight armor
MC.not_for: Standard MC not in Class I Div 1

MI.use: Fire alarm, emergency systems, extreme high temp, hazardous
MI.caution: MgO is hygroscopic — seal ends immediately after cutting

TC.TC-ER: Can run exposed up to 50 ft between tray and equipment

instrument_cable.shield: Ground at ONE end only to prevent ground loops

RMC.where_required: Hazardous areas (Div 1 and 2), physical damage, outdoor industrial

EMT.where_used: Indoor dry, non-hazardous, control rooms, electrical rooms
EMT.NOT_permitted: Hazardous locations, direct burial, outdoor industrial

PVC_coated_RMC.note: Touch up all cut ends and thread exposure

PVC.expansion: 4 inches per 100 ft per 100F temp change

LFMC.length_limit: 6 ft max for grounding, 3 ft in hazardous
LFMC.caution: Standard flex fittings NOT interchangeable with liquidtight

316SS.resists: Chlorides, dilute sulfuric acid, organics, caustics

Monel_400.use: HF alkylation units, seawater — NON-NEGOTIABLE for HF alky

copper.NOT_for: Process impulse lines, ammonia service, H2S service

common_material_mistakes: Using 304SS where 316SS specified — chloride cracking in service
Wrong conduit seal compound in hazardous location — inspector rejects
Mixing aluminum and steel conduit without dielectric unions — galvanic corrosion
EMT outdoors in industrial — corrosion and code violation
Forgetting anti-seize on stainless fittings — galling and stuck fittings
Wrong gasket material for service — discovered during hydro or in operation
Standard PVC cement on CPVC (or vice versa) — joint failure
Not reaming conduit after cutting — insulation damage during wire pull
